key_insight: XCT-SAM improves industrial XCT defect segmentation by adapting SAM in two stages with parameter-efficient Conv-LoRA, bridging the gap from natural images to microstructural defects.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a practical, low-training-cost path to deploy foundation models for additive manufacturing quality control where labeled XCT data are scarce.
method: First adapt SAM on alloy microstructure images to learn materials-domain features.
Then transfer the adapted model to XCT defect data for sequential domain bridging.
Use Conv-LoRA with rank r=2 to train about 4.15M parameters while freezing over 99% of the model.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on out-of-distribution CycleGAN-XCT benchmarks and real NIST XCT scans, where it achieved the best overall IoU and Dice versus zero-shot SAM and other adapted baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and similarity of the intermediate alloy-microstructure dataset, so the adaptation pipeline could be sensitive to domain selection.
paper_title: XCT-SAM: Sequential Parameter-Efficient Domain Adaptation of SAM for Industrial XCT Defect Segmentation
